title: Sendivogius' New Chemical Light: The Enigma of the Sages and the Alchemical Flood
subtitle: Michael Sendivogius' Parable or Enigma of the Sages — the Solar tree, the Mercurial water, the island of the Hesperides, and the resurrection of the Phoenix through putrefaction

summary.one_line: Sendivogius' allegorical vision of Neptune, Saturn, the Solar and Lunar trees, and the water that does not wet the hands — the alchemical process of putrefaction, generation of the Salamander, and ...
summary.scope: The full text of Sendivogius' 'Enigma of the Sages' (from the Tractatus de Lapide Philosophorum, 1604), containing the allegorical vision of the island in the southern hemisphere, the seven trees (Solar and Lunar), the ten parts of the water, Saturn's instruction on putrefaction, and Neptune's revelation of the generation of the Phoenix and the multiplication of the Stone.

sources.label: Sendivogius, Enigma of the Sages — from Tractatus de Lapide Philosophorum (1604), included in the Alchemy Website

body.text: I saw ancient Neptune, with a trident in his hand, rise from our sea, who after a friendly salutation, carried me to a most beautiful island situated in the southern hemisphere. Concealed under a rock, Neptune shewed me two minerals of that island, gold and chalybs (steel). Then I was conducted to an orchard planted with a great variety of beautiful trees. Among these he shewed me seven enriched by particular names; and two of them towered above the rest. One bore fruit which shone like the sun, and its leaves resembled gold; the fruit of the other was whiter than lilies, and its leaves were like fine silver. Neptune called the first the Solar, and the second the Lunar tree. The only thing which it was difficult to obtain in the island was water. The inhabitants had tried to get it from a spring but the only water that could be drunk was that condensed out of the rays of the sun and moon. No one could attract more than ten parts of this water.

'Lord, I behold here a marvellous thing. The water is small in quantity; nevertheless, the fruit of this tree is consumed therein by a gentle heat.' He graciously replied: 'My son, it is true that this thing is wonderful. But this water is the water of life, and has such power to exalt the qualities of this fruit, that it shall afterwards, without sowing or planting — only by its fragrance — transmute the six trees which remain into its own nature. Moreover, this water is as a woman to the fruit: the fruits of this tree can putrefy nowhere but in this water; and though the fruit by itself be wonderful and precious — yet when it putrefies in this water, it brings forth out of this putrefaction a Salamander that endures the fire; its blood is more precious than all treasures, and has power to render fertile six trees such as you see here.'

Neptune said: 'What you say about the act of generation is true; and yet you know that worms are produced in a different way from quadrupeds, namely by putrefaction, and the place or earth in which this putrefaction occurs is feminine. In our substance the Mother is the water of which so much has been said, and its offspring is produced by putrefaction, after the manner of worms. Hence the Sages call it the Phoenix and Salamander. Its generation is a resurrection rather than a birth, and for this reason it is immortal or indestructible. Now, whatsoever is conceived of two bodies is subject to the law of death; but the life of this fruit is a separation from all that is corruptible about it. It is the same with the Phoenix, which separates of itself from its corruptible body.'

Sendivogius' two trees — Solar and Lunar — are the ida and pingala nadis of the subtle body, the two lateral channels that rise from the muladhara to the ajna. The Solar tree (pingala, surya-nadi) bears fruit that 'shines like the sun' — the active, heating, masculine current; the Lunar tree (ida, chandra-nadi) bears fruit 'whiter than lilies' — the receptive, cooling, feminine current. The seven trees total are the seven chakras, with the Solar and Lunar towering above the rest as the two dominant currents that must be balanced. The water 'condensed out of the rays of the sun and moon' is the amrita that is formed when the two currents meet in the ajna and their essences 'condense' into the nectar that drips down into the heart. The ten parts of the water are the ten vayus (pranic airs) of the subtle body, of which only one (the apana, the 'watery' current of elimination) is the 'water that does not wet the hands' — the secret principle that the Layayoga calls the 'apana-agni,' the fire of elimination that is the hidden transformative agent. In Steiner's science of the etheric body, the ten parts are the ten 'formative forces' that work in the human organism, corresponding to the ten sephiroth of the Cabalistic tree.

The putrefaction of the Solar fruit in the water — producing the Salamander that endures the fire — is the alchemical description of the kundalini's transformation through the chakras. The 'water' that is the mother is the apana-vayu (the eliminative energy) in which the solar consciousness (the 'fruit') must 'putrefy' — i.e., undergo the death of the limited ego — before it can generate the 'Salamander' (the kundalini-fire) that 'endures the fire' of higher consciousness. The Salamander that lives in fire and is not consumed is the kundalini-shakti herself, who is said to be 'cool as the moon yet burning as ten million suns' (Yogini-hridaya). The 'blood' of the Salamander that is 'more precious than all treasures' is the amrita that flows from the crown chakra when the serpent has attained the sahasrara — the 'blood of the Stone' that is the elixir of both physical longevity and spiritual liberation. In the PGM, this is the 'blood of the god' that the theurgist drinks in the ritual of deification — the pneuma that is the vehicle of the daimon (PGM IV.475-829). The 'fragrance' that alone transmutes the six trees is the subtle influence of the perfected yogi that radiates without effort — the gandha (fragrance) of the jivan-mukta that is described in the Yogavasistha as the sign of the liberated one.

The Phoenix that 'separates of itself from its corruptible body' and is 'immortal or indestructible' because its generation 'is a resurrection rather than a birth' is the daimonic vehicle — the augoeides (the radiant body) of the Iamblichean theurgy. In the Tantraloka, this is the shuddha-deha (the pure body) that is formed when the kundalini has reached the sahasrara and the practitioner's subtle vehicle is 'fixed' in the light of the absolute. The doctrine that what is 'conceived of two bodies is subject to the law of death, but the life of this fruit is a separation from all that is corruptible about it' is the precise distinction between the lower birth (through the parents, through the union of sperm and egg, subject to death) and the higher birth (through regeneration, through the alchemical marriage of the two principles within, leading to immortality). This is the same distinction that the Corpus Hermeticum (CH XIII) makes between the 'ten torments of matter' (the corruptible body) and the 'tetrad of powers' that are born in the ogdoad (the regenerated body). Steiner calls this the 'resurrection body' — the transformed etheric body that the disciple must build through the conscious work of self-transformation, the 'stone that the builders rejected' that becomes the 'head of the corner.' The Phoenix rising from its ashes is the soul's liberation from the cycle of reincarnation, the kaivalya of the Yoga-sutras.

gaps: The 'New Chemical Light' broader treatise and the accompanying Parabola are not yet extracted
The multiplication series — from 1 to 10 to 100 to 1000 to 10,000 — needs commentary on the alchemical geometry of increase

issues.title: Passages are extracts from a short allegory
issues.description: The Enigma is concise. Full verbatim text is included but the broader Sendivogius corpus (the New Chemical Light, the Parabola) should be referenced.

versions.summary: Initial RO created from Sendivogius' Enigma of the Sages. Six passages cover the island and trees, Saturn's instruction, and the Phoenix generation, with commentary connecting to the nadi system, kundalini transformation, and the augoeides resurrection body.

timeline.event: RO created in draft state from Sendivogius' Enigma of the Sages
